title: viola banksii
intro: Viola banksii, also known as the purple viola or Banks' violet, is a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used traditionally in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and supporting immune system function. Viola banksii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory issues.
